definitions.text: To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
examples: She stirred the pudding with a spoon.
He stirred his coffee so the sugar wouldn't stay at the bottom.

definitions.text: To disturb the content of (a container) by passing an object through it.
examples: Would you please stir this pot so that the chocolate doesn't burn?

definitions.text: To emotionally affect; to touch, to move.
The act or result of stirring (moving around the particles of a liquid etc.)
examples: Can you give the soup a little stir?

definitions.text: agitation; tumult; bustle; noise or various movements.
Public disturbance or commotion; tumultuous disorder; seditious uproar.

examples: He's going to be spending maybe ten years in stir.
